description:
GOAL: Optimize hot calls by fusing adjacent frames into one Cranelift fn — no
allocation for fused calls. Performance-load-bearing for v1.

## Decisions captured

- IN SCOPE for v1, NOT deferred (user decision: "i think we should inline").
  Naive frame-per-call is too slow for tight loops.
- Operate on fz-IR BEFORE Cranelift lowering. Module-level pass: in-place
  rewrites of caller FnIrs. Produces a "merged" caller whose body absorbs the
  callee's blocks (renumbered) directly.
- Static decision (NOT tier-up-driven). Decided once at codegen time.
- New module: src/ir_inline.rs. Public entry: `pub fn inline_module(m: &mut Module)`.
- Pipeline placement: inside compile_with_backend, AFTER
  rewrite_known_target_closures (ir_codegen.rs:1718), BEFORE the final
  type_module call (ir_codegen.rs:1719). rewrite_known_target_closures runs
  first and demotes known-target CallClosure/TailCallClosure to Call/TailCall.
  The final type_module still runs after inlining, so the typer sees fused
  bodies.
- v0 call-shape scope: TailCall and Call+Cont only. Closure-call inlining
  (the original Cases 3-4) is eliminated: rewrite_known_target_closures
  (fz-ul4.29.10.3) already converts known-target closures to direct
  Call/TailCall before the inliner runs. Remaining CallClosure/TailCallClosure
  are dynamically dispatched and cannot be statically inlined.
- v0 heuristic: leaf callees only, <= 8 IR stmts (user decision 2026-05-11).

## Architecture (researched 2026-05-11, updated 2026-05-15)

fz-IR is in CPS form (see src/fz_ir.rs). Two callsite shapes are in scope:

  1. Term::TailCall { callee: FnId, args }
       Caller forwards its continuation; callee receives our cont_ptr.
       Runtime (emit_tail_call, ir_codegen.rs:4829): if self_id == callee_id,
       overwrite frame slots in place; else alloc fresh.

  2. Term::Call { callee: FnId, args, continuation: Cont { fn_id, captured } }
       For uniform callers: allocates a cont frame and callee frame (emit_call,
       ir_codegen.rs:4680). For native callers: builds a cont closure object
       (build_cont_closure, ir_codegen.rs:3469) and issues return_call. Both
       paths use the Cont.fn_id as the next-frame identity.

NOT in scope for the inliner:
- Term::CallClosure / TailCallClosure: known-target ones demoted by
  rewrite_known_target_closures; remainder are dynamic.
- Term::Receive: scheduler yield point. A fn whose body contains Receive MUST
  retain a real frame for process suspension/resume.

Continuations are themselves real FnIrs in the module — produced by a unique
cps_split_call per call site (ir_lower.rs:1039). A continuation's entry block
params are [result_param, cap_params...] — result FIRST, then captured.

Lambda entry params: [captured..., callsite_args...] (ir_lower.rs:868).

## Inlining semantics by case

  Case 1 (TailCall):
    - Renumber callee's blocks and vars (alpha_rename).
    - Caller's `TailCall(F, args)` becomes `Goto(F_entry', args)`.
    - F's `Return(v)` stays `Return(v')` (caller's cont is unchanged).
    - F's `Halt(v)` stays `Halt(v')`.

  Case 2 (Call+Cont):
    - Renumber.
    - Caller's `Call(F, args) -> Cont{K, captured}` becomes
      `Goto(F_entry', args)`.
    - F's `Return(v)` becomes `TailCall(K, [v', captured...])`. K is the
      continuation FnIr. Calling it via TailCall is operationally identical
      to the runtime's cont mechanism.
    - F's `Halt(v)` stays `Halt(v')`.

## Case 2 safety w.r.t. cont_fns analysis

  codegen's cont_fns analysis (ir_codegen.rs:1901-1917) assigns a special
  native signature `(result, self) tail` to every fn appearing in any
  Cont.fn_id field. The inliner rewrites `Return(v') -> TailCall(K, ...)`,
  which removes K from the continuation position.

  This is safe because each K is produced by a unique cps_split_call
  (ir_lower.rs:1039 mints a fresh FnId per call site). K appears in exactly
  ONE Term::Call.continuation.fn_id. After inlining, K has zero cont_fn
  references. On the next compilation:
    - K is no longer in cont_fns -> gets regular native sig
    - K's entry params are [result, cap_params...] (unchanged by inliner)
    - TailCall(K, [v', captured...]) provides exactly those args
  Correctness confirmed: no ABI mismatch.

  If K is itself leaf and inlinable (common for short continuations), the
  fixpoint loop will inline K too in a subsequent iteration.

## Why "leaf" is the right v0 heuristic

A leaf fn has only Goto/If/Return/Halt terminators — NO Call/TailCall/
CallClosure/TailCallClosure/Receive inside its body:

  - Cannot reintroduce recursion.
  - Direct self-recursion (callee.id == caller.id) also guarded.
  - Term::Receive explicitly excluded: inlining a fn that suspends would
    corrupt the process continuation chain.
  - Keeps frame schemas valid: ir_codegen builds schemas from entry-block
    param count (build_frame_schema, ir_codegen.rs:732). Inlining doesn't
    change any fn's entry params.

## Module.fn_idx

Module now carries fn_idx: HashMap<FnId, usize> for O(1) FnId lookup (see
fz_ir.rs). The inliner modifies existing FnIrs in place (appending blocks);
it does NOT add new FnIrs to module.fns. Therefore fn_idx never needs to be
updated by inline_module. BUT: the inliner must mutate a caller's FnIr while
holding an immutable reference to the callee's FnIr — implementation must
index the caller by fn_idx then borrow separately, not hold both at once.

## Frame schema interaction

ir_codegen builds frame schemas from entry-block param count (ir_codegen.rs:732).
Inlining doesn't change any fn's entry params, so no schema recomputation needed.
ir_liveness.rs was deleted in fz-ul4.11.30; the comment in fz_ir.rs about
"liveness populating frame_schema_id" is stale documentation.

## SourceInfo side-table

Module.source (SourceInfo) maps Var.0 to source spans. Alpha-rename mints new
Vars; those vars get DUMMY spans. Acceptable — ir_typer uses SourceInfo for
diagnostics only, not correctness.

## Configuration

  pub const INLINE_BUDGET: usize = 8;     // total Stmt::Let cap on callee
  pub const MAX_ITERATIONS: usize = 3;    // fixpoint cap
  pub const GROWTH_CAP: usize = 4;        // per-fn post-inline <= 4x original

## Children (in dependency order)

  - fz-ul4.11.15.1 — Inliner skeleton + leaf detector + alpha-rename primitives
  - fz-ul4.11.15.2 — TailCall inlining (Case 1)
  - fz-ul4.11.15.3 — Call+Cont inlining (Case 2)
  - fz-ul4.11.15.5 — Fixpoint + bloat cap + pipeline integration
  - fz-ul4.11.15.6 — Hot-loop assert + parity + ticket closeout

(fz-ul4.11.15.4 closed: closure-call inlining eliminated by
rewrite_known_target_closures in fz-ul4.29.10.3.)

## Tests (rolled up across children)

  - Hot loops show no per-iter allocation after inlining.
  - GC still finds correct roots in inlined code.
  - Inliner doesn't introduce code bloat beyond GROWTH_CAP per fn.
  - ir_interp parity: inlined module produces same values as un-inlined module.

## Risks

  - Inliner correctness: must not violate liveness invariants or frame
    layouts. Mitigation: ir_interp parity test on every change.
  - Inliner too aggressive: code bloat -> instruction cache pressure.
    Mitigation: GROWTH_CAP + MAX_ITERATIONS.
  - Forgetting to remap a nested Var (BitFieldIr::size, etc.).
    Mitigation: exhaustive Prim walk in alpha_rename; targeted test per variant.

## Deferred (tracked as explicit follow-up tickets)

  - fz-ul4.11.26 — @no_inline attribute (per-fn opt-out)
  - fz-ul4.11.27 — Cross-module inlining
  - fz-ul4.11.28 — Bounded recursion unrolling
  - fz-ul4.11.29 — Dead-callee DCE pass

## References

  - src/fz_ir.rs               — Prim (190-239), Term (254-293), Cont (248-252),
                                  FnIr/Module (304-404), fn_idx (387-391)
  - src/ir_lower.rs:1039       — cps_split_call (one fresh FnId per call site)
  - src/ir_lower.rs:929        — cps_split_call_closure
  - src/ir_lower.rs:868        — lower_lambda ([captured..., args...] entry params)
  - src/ir_codegen.rs:732      — build_frame_schema
  - src/ir_codegen.rs:1491     — compile_with_backend entry
  - src/ir_codegen.rs:1718-19  — rewrite_known_target_closures + final type_module
                                  (pipeline insertion point)
  - src/ir_codegen.rs:1901-17  — cont_fns analysis
  - src/ir_codegen.rs:3185     — CodegenEnv struct
  - src/ir_codegen.rs:3469     — build_cont_closure (native-path cont allocation)
  - src/ir_codegen.rs:3513     — emit_terminator (main dispatch)
  - src/ir_codegen.rs:4680     — emit_call
  - src/ir_codegen.rs:4829     — emit_tail_call
  - src/ir_interp.rs:231       — call/return semantics (Term::Call branch)
  - src/spec_registry.rs       — SpecRegistry (extracted in fz-cg2.8)
title: Frame inliner (performance optimization)
